assessment: STRONGEST candidate of the search. A main-sequence star (no IR excess, W1-W2~0) fading ~15%% MONOTONICALLY and ACHROMATICALLY (g and r) over 7.4 yr with 791 epochs; not a known variable; the only fader in its 1.3-deg field, so not a per-CCD systematic. Resists the dusty-disk, single-band, slow-variable-giant, and shared-epoch explanations. A genuine slow-dimming star worthy of independent follow-up before any claim.
followups: Gaia DR3 full row: parallax/distance, RUWE (binarity), phot_variable_flag, proper motion (blend-motion test)
cross-survey confirmation: ASAS-SN / ATLAS light curve (15%% fade is detectable at V~16)
